title: Market Brief — Friday, May 15, 2026
summary: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100s at 70%, and MI300X at 60%, while B200s at 25% signal scarcity and GB200s at 10% availability mark a severe shortage with no transparent pricing. The biggest price signal is B200 spot at $4.20/hr median — the only next-gen chip where inference margins turn positive at high-tier pricing, making it the bottleneck everyone is bidding on.
paragraphs: GPU supply is bifurcated: A100s sit at 85% availability (oversupply), H100s at 70%, and MI300X at 60%, while B200s at 25% signal scarcity and GB200s at 10% availability mark a severe shortage with no transparent pricing. The biggest price signal is B200 spot at $4.20/hr median — the only next-gen chip where inference margins turn positive at high-tier pricing, making it the bottleneck everyone is bidding on.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), Llama 70B inference runs $9.48/M on A100, $9.84/M on H100, and $11.90/M on MI300X — meaning any provider selling open-weight model inference under $10/M is burning cash. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output and Meta Llama 4 Maverick at $0.60/M are selling at losses exceeding $9/M tokens, subsidizing adoption with venture dollars. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

Overvalued: o3 Pro at $80/M output carries a 95%+ margin on B200s and prices in scarcity that competition will erode as B200 supply loosens past 25% availability. Undervalued: B200 spot compute at $4.20/hr is the only GPU class offering positive inference margins at standard pricing tiers — accumulate B200 capacity now before GB200 shortages push more demand downstream.
bullets: 85% availability on A100s signals oversupply, making legacy compute a buyer's market.
10% availability on GB200s marks a severe shortage with no public spot pricing established yet.
$0.378 per million output tokens makes DeepSeek V3.2 the cheapest frontier-adjacent model, sold at an estimated $9.10/M token loss versus TCO.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means API pricing has fallen roughly 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline of 100.
$4.02 per million tokens on B200 is the lowest raw inference cost, 47% cheaper than MI300X at $9.52/M.
25% B200 availability sits exactly at the scarcity threshold, with spot ranges spanning $3.50 to $5.50 per hour.
